step_by_step_reasoning: We identify two distinct user actors on the left: 'Public' users and 'Developer/Newsroom' users. The core flow routes public traffic through an origin proxy ECS cluster, which fetches static resources from S3 or pre-rendered content from S3. On cache miss, it routes to a Lambda rendering engine. The Lambda engine interacts with DynamoDB for templates, and with a secondary content proxy ECS cluster to fetch content. The content proxy checks ElastiCache and falls back to an external CMS cloud.

graph.name: Arc Publishing Web Rendering Platform
graph.notes: Web rendering platform for Washington Post and other media companies. Uses multi-tier caching (CDN, S3 pre-rendered pages, and ElastiCache for CMS JSON data) to guarantee high performance and availability.

nodes.name: Public Users
nodes.notes: Access the website to view HTML pages like WashingtonPost.com.

nodes.name: Developer & Newsroom Users
nodes.notes: Create page templates and deploy rendering code.

nodes.name: Origin ECS
nodes.notes: Handles traffic routing and origin proxying.

nodes.name: Static Resources
nodes.notes: Stores static assets like CSS and JavaScript.

nodes.name: Pre-Rendered Content
nodes.notes: Stores pre-rendered HTML content for faster delivery.

nodes.name: Rendering Lambda
nodes.notes: Performs dynamic HTML page rendering when cache is missed.

nodes.name: Content Proxy ECS
nodes.notes: Proxies requests to external and internal CMS systems.

nodes.name: Template Store
nodes.notes: Stores page builder templates and configurations.

nodes.name: Raw JSON Cache
nodes.notes: Caches raw CMS JSON payloads with a TTL.

nodes.name: CMS & APIs
nodes.notes: External content management systems and APIs.

edges.notes: Falls back to dynamic Lambda rendering on cache miss.
Lambda requests content details from content proxy.
Checks ElastiCache for raw JSON content.
Fetches raw data from backend CMS systems.
Newsroom configures templates stored in DynamoDB.
Retrieves page layouts and templates during execution.
Saves rendered output back to S3 pre-render store.
